creator: marklmi26-fbsd@yahoo.com
text:
(In reply to Mark Linimon from comment #9)

What I quoted from Konstantin Belousov in Comment #7 indicates
that the issue is:

) Real behavior for FreeBSD in general.

) Expected behavior that is not going to be avoided
  in the future.

I tend to reference Comment #7 in this bugzilla when
suggesting folks avoid using swapfile based swap space.
That would not be as effective with the "Overcome By Events"
classification. Similarly for various other available
classifications.

If there was a "no plan to fix/improve" classification, I'd
suggest using it.

But, as stands, just leaving this open seems best to me.
However, if you or someone decides to do otherwise, I'll
leave whatever is done alone. There is no great selection
for this as things are.

I've also changed version to just CURRENT (no version
indicated) so it does not need repeated updates over time
in order to avoid looking out of date. (There is no
alternative for "all modern versions and, likely, all
future versions".)
